title: YouTube node does not accept binary/octet-stream
symptom: The YouTube node returns an error stating 'Media type 'binary/octet-stream' is not supported.' despite the input showing as video/mp4.
applies_when: Input file is incorrectly recognized as binary/octet-stream
not_this_if: Input file is correctly recognized as video/mp4
root_cause: The YouTube node requires the input file to be in a supported video format. If the file is downloaded as 'binary/octet-stream', it may not be properly recognized as video/mp4, leading to the error.
fix_steps: Install ffmpeg in the Docker container running n8n using 'docker exec' command.
Use ffmpeg to convert the downloaded file to the correct video format.
Store the converted file in a temporary folder within the n8n instance.
Read the converted file again for further processing in the YouTube node.
agent_instruction: Install ffmpeg in your n8n Docker container and convert the video file to the correct format.
